changes.field: Front matter — all 5 required SOP v1.3.1 fields valid
changes.finding: All five mandatory front matter fields present and correctly valued. MIXED/STANDARD classification is correct: sprint adds DTO fields derived from existing deterministic rail state and makes frontend surface/label changes only — no scoring logic, signal evaluation, root-cause, or Knowledge Bus assets touched. TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH is appropriate for a STANDARD MIXED sprint.

changes.field: MIXED/STANDARD risk classification correct — escalation boundary verified
changes.finding: This sprint extends the ConsumerDomainScoreV1 DTO with two integer fields derived from existing rail/missing-marker data, remaps frontend label strings, and repositions a hidden component. None of these actions modify Intelligence Core components (scoring logic, signal evaluators, root-cause, Knowledge Bus, IDL records, pipeline output construction beyond the explicit DTO additions). The STANDARD classification holds. Escalation to HIGH is required only if any of the nine explicit STOP conditions in the prompt are triggered during implementation.
citations: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 18-37: Classification and escalation conditions

changes.field: Frontend component at stated path — content verified (Stage 2A)
changes.finding: Wave1DomainCards.tsx exists and content cross-referenced against sprint requirements. Key findings: bandLabelDisplay() at line 16 maps 'review' → 'Needs review' (sprint must change to 'Needs attention' per agreed vocabulary). tierLabel() at line 26 maps 'high' → 'High confidence' (sprint must change to 'Good reliability'). No evidence_completeness fields rendered. No plain_english_descriptor rendered. Component renders consumer_label (line 76), score as percentage (line 66), band_label via bandLabelDisplay() (line 88), confidence_tier via tierLabel() (line 90), headline_sentence (line 91). Card correctly does NOT render clinical_label in the consumer view.

changes.field: Assembler rail constants and block functions — content verified (Stage 2A)
changes.finding: All three block functions and critical constants confirmed in domain_score_assembler.py. _CV_RAIL_BIOMARKERS at line 56 contains exactly 5 markers: total_cholesterol, ldl_cholesterol, hdl_cholesterol, triglycerides, tc_hdl_ratio. _MET_CORE at line 65: (glucose, hba1c). _MET_ENHANCERS at line 66: (insulin, triglycerides). _HEP_CONFIDENCE_POOL at line 68: 9 markers (alt, ast, ggt, alp, albumin, total_bilirubin, bilirubin, total_protein, globulin). _missing_for_rail() at line 324 reads hss[system_key]['missing_biomarkers'] from the scoring engine. cv_block() at line 400, met_block() at line 453, liv_block() at line 505. All three blocks call ConsumerDomainScoreV1(...) directly and must be updated to include new fields.
All six cross-sprint guard regression test modules named in the Required Validation Commands block confirmed on disk.

sentinel/packs/escaped_defects_v1.json confirmed present. Sprint must add the five named Sentinel defect classes (health_system_card_hidden_from_main_journey, health_system_card_frontend_calculates_evidence_completeness, health_system_card_clinical_label_visible, health_system_card_subsystem_placeholder_visible, health_system_card_internal_language_visible). No placeholder Sentinel entries permitted — each class must point to an active deterministic test.

changes.finding: Forbidden list includes backend/ssot/, backend/core/scoring/, backend/core/pipeline/, backend/core/units/, backend/core/analytics/root_cause*, backend/core/analytics/report_compiler*, knowledge_bus/, automation_bus/state/*, and the three control-plane scripts. Allowed files are correctly scoped: results/page.tsx (placement), Wave1DomainCards.tsx (labels, fields), analysis.ts (DTO type), backend/core/models/results.py (Pydantic model), domain_score_assembler.py (new fields in three block functions), domain_narrative_wave1.py (prose cleanup only if tightly scoped). Allowed and forbidden sets are disjoint. No overlap detected.

changes.finding: Token missing/mismatched → STOP with exact error text. Any controlling authority file missing → STOP. Baseline assumptions false (Wave1DomainCards.tsx absent, consumer_domain_scores absent, Wave 1 domain IDs absent, cards already prominent) → STOP and report. Escalation trigger conditions (marker sets, scoring policy, confidence rules, Intelligence Core outputs beyond explicit DTO additions) → escalate to HIGH and STOP. Forbidden path required → STOP.
All commands use python -m pytest, npm run type-check, git, and Test-Path. No bash-only constructs. Compatible with Windows PowerShell.

changes.field: Evidence completeness denominator: no _MET_RAIL_BIOMARKERS constant exists — Cursor must create it
changes.finding: _CV_RAIL_BIOMARKERS exists as a 5-element tuple at line 56. No equivalent _MET_RAIL_BIOMARKERS constant exists — the metabolic domain uses separate _MET_CORE (glucose, hba1c) and _MET_ENHANCERS (insulin, triglycerides). Cursor must define _MET_RAIL_BIOMARKERS = _MET_CORE + _MET_ENHANCERS (4 markers) as a new constant above the block functions, use len(_MET_RAIL_BIOMARKERS) as the metabolic denominator, and use (len(_MET_RAIL_BIOMARKERS) - len(missing)) as the numerator. This is derivable entirely from existing constants — no expected marker sets may be changed.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking. Cursor instruction: add _MET_RAIL_BIOMARKERS = _MET_CORE + _MET_ENHANCERS immediately after line 66. denominator = len(_MET_RAIL_BIOMARKERS). numerator = denominator - len(missing_for_met).

changes.field: Evidence completeness denominator: liver domain expected set is augmented beyond scoring rail — Cursor must define explicit constant
changes.finding: For liver, _missing_for_rail(hss, _RAIL_LIVER) reads from scoring_policy.yaml liver rail (audit confirms: alt, ast only). But liv_block() at lines 518-527 manually augments the missing list with ast, ggt, alp, albumin, and bilirubin when absent from panel. The effective expected set used by the assembler is therefore {alt, ast, ggt, alp, albumin, bilirubin} — 6 markers. If Cursor derives the denominator from the scoring rail alone (2 markers: alt, ast), it will be too narrow and inconsistent with the actual missing_marker_ids list emitted. Cursor must define _LIV_DOMAIN_BIOMARKERS as a new constant representing the effective expected set: ('alt', 'ast', 'ggt', 'alp', 'albumin', 'bilirubin'). Use len(_LIV_DOMAIN_BIOMARKERS) = 6 as the denominator. Numerator = denominator - number of _LIV_DOMAIN_BIOMARKERS markers absent from panel.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking. Cursor instruction: add _LIV_DOMAIN_BIOMARKERS = ('alt', 'ast', 'ggt', 'alp', 'albumin', 'bilirubin') near _HEP_CONFIDENCE_POOL at line 68. Do NOT use len(_HEP_CONFIDENCE_POOL) = 9 — that includes total_bilirubin, total_protein, globulin which are not universally expected. denominator = len(_LIV_DOMAIN_BIOMARKERS) = 6. numerator = 6 - len([m for m in _LIV_DOMAIN_BIOMARKERS if m not in panel_biomarker_ids]).

changes.field: ConsumerDomainScoreV1 uses extra='forbid' — new fields must have defaults to avoid breaking test fixtures
changes.finding: The Pydantic model has extra='forbid' and frozen=True. Adding required fields (Field(...)) will break all existing instantiation sites including tests. New fields must use Field(default=None) with Optional[int] type, or Field(default=0) with int type. Optional[int] = Field(default=None) is recommended: the frontend can suppress evidence completeness display when both values are None, and existing test data will not fail. The TypeScript interface must use the optional pattern (evidence_completeness_numerator?: number | null) to match.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking. Cursor instruction: declare evidence_completeness_numerator: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, ...) and evidence_completeness_denominator: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, ...) in the Pydantic model. Match with evidence_completeness_numerator?: number | null in the TypeScript interface. Frontend must render the evidence completeness display only when both values are non-null integers.

changes.field: plain_english_descriptor — frontend static map is lower-risk than backend DTO addition
changes.finding: The DOMAIN-UX1 audit (section 9, 'Frontend-only safe now') explicitly recommends implementing plain_english_descriptor as a frontend DOMAIN_DESCRIPTOR constant map since there are exactly three fixed Wave 1 domain IDs. This avoids a DTO/Pydantic/TypeScript update for a purely presentational field. The prompt allows either approach. The frontend static map approach is correct governance: it is strictly presentational, the domain IDs are fixed constants for Wave 1, and it does not become interpretation logic. If Cursor chooses the backend field approach, it must add the field to both the Pydantic model and the TypeScript interface and populate it in all three block functions.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking. Recommendation: use frontend DOMAIN_DESCRIPTOR constant map in Wave1DomainCards.tsx. If backend DTO field is chosen, ensure both Pydantic model and TypeScript interface are updated and all three blocks emit the value.

changes.field: band_label 'review' vocabulary: backend value vs frontend display string
changes.finding: Backend _band_label_from_0_100() at line 87 returns 'review' for scores below 45. The agreed UX vocabulary is 'Needs attention'. Current frontend bandLabelDisplay() at line 22 maps 'review' → 'Needs review'. Sprint must change this to 'review' → 'Needs attention'. This is a frontend-only string change — the backend band value 'review' must NOT be changed. No backend change required.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking. Cursor instruction: in Wave1DomainCards.tsx line 22, change review: 'Needs review' to review: 'Needs attention'. Backend unchanged.

changes.field: DTO sync requirement — Pydantic and TypeScript must be updated in the same sprint
changes.finding: The backend ConsumerDomainScoreV1 Pydantic model (backend/core/models/results.py:167) and the TypeScript interface (frontend/app/types/analysis.ts:328) must remain in sync. If Cursor adds evidence_completeness_numerator and evidence_completeness_denominator to the Pydantic model but forgets to update the TypeScript interface (or vice versa), type-check will pass on one side but the frontend will receive untyped data and evidence completeness cannot be rendered safely. Both files must be updated together.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking. Cursor must treat both files as a single atomic update for any DTO field addition.
